steps.title: Tuck Him Away
steps.text: The king's worst enemies are open files and diagonals leading to him. Castling gets him out of the center into a fortress of pawns. Keep that fortress intact — don't rush to push your castled-side pawns unless you have a concrete reason.

steps.hint: Short castling — the most common choice.

steps.title: After Castling
steps.text: You're not done! Keep pawns in front: f2, g2, h2. The knight on f3 defends h2. Don't play g3 or h3 unless necessary — every pawn move near your king creates a weakness for later.
